title: Implement shared-ref retention, compaction, and bounded-history policy
summary: Add the operational controls the design calls for so shared coordination refs remain compact over time: bounded recent history, explicit compaction points, hot-versus-cold state separation, and rules for retaining enough evidence without turning the shared ref into an unbounded append-log pain point.

acceptance.label: The live shared ref remains compact enough for daily fetch and publish workflows even as coordination history grows.
Compaction and retention preserve the evidence needed for correctness and audit without keeping the live head bloated forever.
